questions.q: In the Ramayana, what is the most distinctive physical feature of Kabandha?
options: He has the body of a fish
questions.explanation: Kabandha is a headless demon, a monstrous torso with his face set in his belly.

questions.q: Who finally slays Kabandha in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama and his brother Lakshmana together kill Kabandha by cutting off his enormous arms.

questions.q: Before his curse, what kind of celestial being was Kabandha?
questions.explanation: Kabandha was originally a gandharva, a celestial being, before he was cursed into a monster.

questions.q: What happens to Kabandha after he is killed by Rama?
options: He is reborn as a demon king
He is freed from his curse
He is imprisoned in the underworld
questions.explanation: Slaying Kabandha releases him from his curse, restoring his celestial form.

questions.q: Toward whom does Kabandha direct Rama and Lakshmana for help?
questions.explanation: Kabandha advises the brothers to seek out Sugriva, the monkey king, as an ally.

questions.q: How does Kabandha try to seize Rama and Lakshmana when they meet him?
questions.explanation: Kabandha grabs the two brothers with his immensely long arms, intending to devour them.

questions.q: During which part of Rama's life does the encounter with Kabandha take place?
questions.explanation: The brothers meet Kabandha while searching for Sita during their forest exile.

questions.q: Where does Kabandha have his face, according to the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Being headless, Kabandha has a huge mouth and face set in his belly.

questions.q: What were Rama and Lakshmana searching for when they encountered Kabandha?
questions.explanation: The brothers were searching for the abducted Sita when they came upon Kabandha.

questions.q: What does Kabandha mainly use his long arms for in the forest?
questions.explanation: Kabandha sweeps up animals and any beings within reach of his arms and eats them.

questions.q: How do Rama and Lakshmana ultimately overcome Kabandha's grip?
options: They burn him with fire arrows
They sever both his arms with their swords
They trick him into releasing them
questions.explanation: Caught in his arms, the brothers cut off both of Kabandha's arms with their swords to free themselves.

questions.q: By whose curse, according to a common account, was Kabandha first made into a deformed monster?
questions.explanation: In Valmiki's account the angry sage Sthulasiras cursed the gandharva into his monstrous, headless shape.

questions.q: How did Kabandha lose his head and have it forced into his body, per the Ramayana?
options: A blow from Indra's thunderbolt drove his head into his trunk
He was decapitated by Rama at birth
questions.explanation: Indra struck him with his thunderbolt (vajra), driving his head and thighs into his body.

questions.q: What was the name of Kabandha in his original celestial existence, before the curses?
questions.explanation: Valmiki names the gandharva as Danu, who through curses became the demon Kabandha.

questions.q: After being struck, how did Kabandha obtain food despite having no head or neck?
options: He grew arms a league long and a mouth in his belly
questions.explanation: Brahma granted him very long arms and a mouth in his stomach so the cursed being could still feed.

questions.q: What did Rama and Lakshmana do with Kabandha's body after killing him, that released his true form?
options: They cremated it on a pyre
They buried it in the river
They cast it from a cliff
They scattered it in the wind
questions.explanation: The brothers cremated Kabandha's body, from which his radiant celestial form arose and ascended.

questions.q: Besides naming Sugriva, what crucial advice does Kabandha give the brothers about approaching the monkey king?
options: To make a pact of friendship with him by fire
To bring him a tribute of gold
To wait a full year before meeting him
questions.explanation: Kabandha counsels Rama to seek Sugriva's friendship, sworn before sacred fire, to gain help finding Sita.

questions.q: To whose hermitage does Kabandha direct Rama on the way to Sugriva, a devoted ascetic awaiting the Lord?
questions.explanation: Kabandha sends Rama toward the ashram of Shabari, the devoted ascetic, near Lake Pampa.

questions.q: Near which lake does Kabandha tell Rama that Sugriva dwells?
questions.explanation: Kabandha describes the route to Lake Pampa, on whose far side Sugriva lives on Mount Rishyamukha.

questions.q: Why was Kabandha unable to regain his celestial form on his own, even though he wished to?
options: His body could only be freed when burned after death by Rama
He had forgotten how to fly
He was bound by chains of iron
He needed a boon from Ravana
questions.explanation: He had been told his deliverance would come only when Rama killed him and his body was cremated.

questions.q: What did Kabandha do to those who wandered into the reach of his arms in the forest?
options: Cursed them to share his fate
Set them free with a warning
questions.explanation: He grabbed and devoured the creatures that strayed within reach of his enormous arms.

questions.q: How did Kabandha come to recognize who Rama and Lakshmana were after seizing them?
options: They told him their identities as he held them
He saw a vision from the gods
He read it in the stars
questions.explanation: While trapped in his grip, the brothers told Kabandha who they were, leading him to recall his prophesied release.

questions.q: Why did the sage Sthulasiras curse the gandharva Danu in the first place, according to the Ramayana?
options: Danu mocked the sage's emaciated, ugly appearance
Danu stole the sage's sacred fire
Danu interrupted the sage's penance with noise
questions.explanation: The proud gandharva ridiculed the deformed ascetic Sthulasiras, who then cursed him to keep that monstrous shape.

questions.q: What austerity or act had earned Danu (Kabandha) a boon of long life that made him reckless toward Indra?
options: Slaying a great demon for the gods
questions.explanation: Having won long life through severe penance, he grew arrogant and even challenged Indra, who struck him down.

questions.q: When Indra drove Kabandha's head into his body, what additional boon did the demon plead for so he would not starve?
options: A mouth and the means to eat
questions.explanation: Since the thunderbolt had crushed his head into his trunk, he begged for a way to eat, and was granted a belly-mouth and long arms.

questions.q: In Valmiki's narrative, in which kanda (book) of the Ramayana does the Kabandha episode primarily occur?
questions.explanation: The slaying of Kabandha falls in the Aranya Kanda, the Forest Book, as the brothers search for Sita.

questions.q: What does Kabandha's restored celestial form do immediately after rising from the funeral pyre?
options: Counsels Rama and ascends to heaven in a chariot
questions.explanation: His shining form rises, gives Rama detailed guidance about Sugriva and Sita, then ascends skyward in a celestial chariot.

questions.q: Which detail about the friendship with Sugriva does Kabandha emphasize so that the alliance will be binding?
options: That the vow be sworn with fire as witness
That it be sealed with a gift of weapons
That it last only one season
That Lakshmana be excluded from it
questions.explanation: Kabandha stresses that Rama and Sugriva should bind their friendship by an oath taken with the sacred fire as witness.

questions.q: According to the account, on which mountain near Pampa does Kabandha say Sugriva is hiding from his brother?
questions.explanation: Kabandha tells Rama that Sugriva dwells on Mount Rishyamukha by Lake Pampa, in fear of his brother Vali.

questions.q: What overall narrative purpose does the Kabandha episode serve in the Ramayana?
options: It bridges the loss of Sita to the alliance with the vanaras
It marks the start of Rama's exile
It describes the building of the bridge to Lanka
It recounts the breaking of Shiva's bow
questions.explanation: Kabandha's guidance turns the grieving brothers toward Sugriva and the monkey allies, advancing the quest to recover Sita.
